name: Chili, Erik Speckman
recipeIngredient: -ground New Mexico chili powder (as much as tastes good, a few teaspoons)
ground New Mexico chili powder (as much as tastes good, a few teaspoons)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover the beans and bay leaf with 2 inches of fresh water in a pot. Bring to a boil.
2. Lower heat and simmer.
3. Toast cumin and oregano over medium heat, stirring so they don't burn. When fragrant, add paprika, cayenne pepper, and chili powders—toast for a few seconds.
4. Remove from heat and grind all ingredients in a mortar and pestle.
5. Dry the chili negro (Ancho) or ancho chili in a hot oven (400°F) for a few minutes. Cool and remove stems, seeds, and veins. Shred and grind again using a food processor, blender, etc.
6. Sauté onions in oil until soft, then add garlic, salt, ground herbs, and chili powder. Cook for 5 minutes.
7. Add tomatoes, their juice, 1 tablespoon chipotle chili, and red wine vinegar. Cook about 20 minutes.
8. Add to the beans and cover with water by 1 inch. Simmer until beans are soft (about 1 hour).
9. Taste and adjust seasoning with cumin, oregano, salt, chili powder, chipotle, and vinegar as needed.
